description_long: This object is a rectangular stone slab with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its upper surface. The grooves create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed dark channels, giving the object a visually striking, high-contrast appearance. The stone appears to be a dense, speckled gray material, possibly granite or a similar igneous rock, with a rough, matte finish. The sides of the slab are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the functional, utilitarian design. The overall form is solid and blocky, suggesting it is intended as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a curb, divider, or anti-vehicle measure. The grooves may serve to increase traction or channel water, enhancing its utility in public spaces.
description: A rectangular, speckled gray stone slab with deep, parallel grooves forming a striped pattern on its top surface. The object is solid, blocky, and visually striking, likely used as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone slab's wide face with horizontal grooves and a solid, blocky profile.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the parallel grooves and the alternating raised and recessed pattern.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same grooved, striped surface and solid form.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep, parallel grooves and the slab's thickness.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or embedded in pavement
attribute_pattern: Regular, parallel grooves creating a striped pattern across the top surface, with high visual contrast between the raised stone and recessed channels.
attribute_weathering: Surface appears freshly cut with minimal weathering, showing a rough, matte finish and no visible signs of erosion or biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts sharply with smoother pavement or concrete, serving as a visual and physical barrier; may be flush with or slightly raised above the surrounding ground.
